document.name: Brooklyn Children's Museum
document.shortDescription: Crown Heights children’s museum (Wed–Sun) with three floors of interactive exhibits and hands-on cultural and science programs for ages about 6 months through 10 years.
document.longDescription:
Brooklyn Children’s Museum describes itself as the world’s first children’s museum and offers three floors of interactive exhibits plus hands-on cultural and science programs for ages about six months through ten years, per the official visit page.

Public hours on the same page: Wednesday–Sunday 10:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m.; last entry 4:50 p.m.; closed Monday–Tuesday.

General admission is $15 per person per the visit page. Members receive free admission and guest discounts as described there. Community Access Thursdays: admission is free every Thursday 2:00–5:00 p.m. (courtesy of Amazon); tickets may be reserved online or at BCM on the day-of; group visits are restricted during that window per the same page. Additional discounted categories are linked from the visit page under tickets/discounts.

Location: 145 Brooklyn Avenue, Brooklyn, NY 11213. Visitor telephone on the visit page: (718) 735-4400 x 151. Visit-related email hello@brooklynkids.org (allow up to 48 hours for a response per the page).

The listing image is an official Brooklyn Children’s Museum program photograph published in BCM WordPress media (Animal Encounters series, photographer credit Winston Williams as shown in the asset filename), re-hosted on ImgBB for ClassScout storage policy compliance.

Sources: https://www.brooklynkids.org/visit/
document.announcementDescription: Free museum admission Thursdays 2–5 p.m.; reserve online or at BCM — see brooklynkids.org/visit.
